channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Homa Bay Woman Dies after Fire Kills Two Children Amid Marital Dispute
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Homa Bay County when a 30-year-old woman, Veronica Akoth, allegedly set her home on fire, killing two of her children and leaving two others critically injured. Neighbour...

item.title: U.S. Air Force program advances next-generation ceramic materials for aerospace and defense applications
item.description: A new research initiative supported by the U.S. Air Force Research Laboratory (AFRL) aims to accelerate the development of next-generation ceramic and ceramic matrix composite (CMC) materials for extr...

item.title: Experts warn Brisbane housing market may face increased downside risks amid tax changes and weaker buyer demand
item.description: A property auction in Brisbane's affluent suburb of Ascot, where a house listed at $3.4 million failed to attract a single bid, has raised concerns about the strength of Australia's housing market. Th...

item.title: A Glimpse of London Life in the Early 1960s Through Vintage Photography
item.description: The early 1960s were a transformative period in London, marking the end of postwar austerity and the beginning of a cultural and social revival. Vintage photos from this era provide an authentic windo...

item.title: Shifting SHEQ from compliance to strategic leadership in modern workplaces
item.description: The article introduces the inaugural edition of SHEQ Management, focusing on the evolving role of Safety, Health, Environment and Quality (SHEQ) in modern industrial environments. It argues that SHEQ ...

item.title: Presidential Commission Begins Legal Process on Election Violence Investigations
item.description: A presidential commission formed to investigate election-related criminal activities has officially started its work and appeared in court, marking an important step in addressing concerns about elect...

item.title: Kenya Finance Bill 2026 proposes VAT exemption on sugarcane transport to cut costs
item.description: Sugarcane farmers and transporters in Kenya are expected to benefit from proposed tax reforms contained in the Finance Bill 2026, which aim to reduce operational costs within the sugar industry. A key...

item.title: Rabada and Klaasen headline strong South African performances in IPL 2026
item.description: South African cricketers delivered a standout showing in the 2026 Indian Premier League (IPL), which concluded on Sunday, with Kagiso Rabada and Heinrich Klaasen leading the charge. Rabada, playing fo...

item.title: Coinbase expands India services to allow rupee-denominated trading
item.description: Coinbase, the US-listed cryptocurrency exchange, has expanded its services in India by enabling rupee-denominated trading. The platform allows users in India to deposit and withdraw funds via the imme...

item.title: EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas visits Islamabad for 8th Pakistan-EU Strategic Dialogue
item.description: The European Union’s top foreign policy official, Kaja Kallas, visited Islamabad on Monday to participate in high-level talks with Pakistan under the 8th EU-Pakistan Strategic Dialogue framework. She ...

item.title: Early-onset dementia diagnosis highlights challenges in accessing support for Australian families
item.description: The article explores the lived experience of Lisa, who was diagnosed with Alzheimer’s disease, a form of dementia, at the age of 47. Her partner, Luke, describes a gradual but noticeable decline in he...

item.title: Daily horoscope predictions for June 1, 2026 across all zodiac signs
item.description: This article presents the daily horoscope for June 1, 2026, offering astrological guidance for each zodiac sign along with a special note for those celebrating their birthday today. The birthday messa...

item.title: Otti announces planned Umuahia Stadium City as Chukwueze visits Abia Government House
item.description: Abia State Governor, Alex Otti, has hosted Super Eagles winger and Abia-born football star, Samuel Chukwueze, at the Government House in Umuahia during a recent visit that also highlighted major devel...

item.title: Tanzania government gains 16% non-dilutable stake in Lindi Jumbo graphite mine via Ndovu Graphite JV
item.description: The Tanzanian government formalized a 16% non-dilutable equity stake in the Lindi Jumbo graphite mine, located in Ruangwa District, Lindi Region, through a joint venture that established Ndovu Graphit...

item.title: First Nations opposition grows over Brisbane 2032 Olympic stadium plans for Victoria Park
item.description: Construction is due to begin on Brisbane’s new Olympic stadium at Victoria Park, a central element of the city’s preparations for the 2032 Olympic and Paralympic Games. However, the project has sparke...

item.title: Kapamilya Love Teams Share Behind-the-Scenes Moments at ASAP XP Big Dome Show

item.title: Ecca Vandal reflects on creative independence and her return with a second album
item.description: Ecca Vandal, the Australian-South African punk and alternative artist, has spoken about her return to music following a period of stepping away from the industry to rediscover her creative identity. A...

item.title: Rick and Morty Season 9 Episode 2 Delivers an Emotional Look at Rick's Inner Conflict
item.description: Rick and Morty Season 9 bounces back strongly in its second episode, 'Ricks Days, Seven Nights,' following a less impressive premiere. The episode delves deeply into the emotional and psychological st...

item.title: Aurat March in Karachi highlights resistance, state hurdles, and demands for women’s rights
item.description: The Aurat March in Karachi, held on May 10, 2026, took place amid significant administrative and political hurdles imposed by authorities. Organisers faced delays in securing the mandatory no-objectio...

item.title: Nigeria faces rising flood risks as losses hit N13 trillion and 2026 threats increase
item.description: Nigeria dey face serious flooding wahala as reports show say states never recover from about N13 trillion losses wey happen between 2024 and 2025. Dis losses cover houses, farms, infrastructure, and s...
